title: Teline eriocarpa
taxonomy: Teline eriocarpa is classified as a flowering plant, specifically belonging to the family Fabaceae, also known as the legume family. It is a genus of plants within the subfamily Faboideae. More specifically, Teline eriocarpa is a part of the tribe Genisteae, which is a group of plants commonly known as the broom or gorse. The classification of Teline eriocarpa also includes the rank of species, indicating it is a distinct species within the genus. intro: Teline eriocarpa, also known as Astragalus crassicarpus, is a perennial legume native to the western regions of North America, characterized by its hairy stems and large, papery seed pods. The health benefits of Teline eriocarpa include its potential to boost the immune system and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable asset in traditional herbal medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include isoflavones, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
